    All Images

    Software for open and reproducible research

    Figure 1

    Image 1 of 1: ‘JSON data file snippet showing EVA/spacewalk data including EVA ID, country, crew members, vehicle type, date of the spacewalk, duration, and purpose’

    Figure 2

    Image 1 of 1: ‘The first few lines of a Python script used as example code for the episode’

    Better start with a software project

    Figure 1

    Image 1 of 1: ‘Diagram for foundational software development workflow with Git showing Git commands and flow of data between components of a Git system, including working directory, staging area, local and remote repository’

    Figure 2

    Image 1 of 1: ‘Two developers can use Git in combination with a remote repository, usually accessed via the internet. By pushing and pulling changes to and from the remote repository, they are each able to synchronise their local copy of the project with the changes the other developer has committed.’

    Figure 3

    Image 1 of 1: ‘Selecting the 'New repository' option from GitHub's dropdown menu labelled '+'’

    Figure 4

    Image 1 of 1: ‘Setting the name of the repository on GitHub through the 'Repository Name' text field’

    Figure 5

    Image 1 of 1: ‘Completing the creation of the GitHub repository by clicking on the 'Create repository' button’

    Figure 6

    Image 1 of 1: ‘Copying the commands to sync the local and remote repositories from the remote repository's home page on GitHub’

    Figure 7

    Image 1 of 1: ‘Home page of a GitHub repository showing all the top-level tabs, including Code, Issues, Pull requests, Actions, projects, Wiki, Insights and Settings’

    Figure 8

    Image 1 of 1: ‘Form for adding an issue on a GitHub repository showing issue title and issue description fields’

    Reproducible software environments

    Figure 1

    Image 1 of 1: ‘A single system might contain multiple virtual environments, each containing a different version of Python and the set of third-party libraries it needs (dependencies) e.g. NumPy, Pandas or Matplotlib. Each environment contains its own complete copy of the required version of each dependency.’

    Figure 2

    Image 1 of 1: ‘Select python interpreter in VS Code’

    Figure 3

    Image 1 of 1: ‘Set interpreter path in VS Code’

    Figure 4

    Image 1 of 1: ‘Showing interpreter status in VS Code status bar’

    Code readability

    Code structure

    Code correctness & testing

    Software documentation

    Software management & collaboration

    Figure 1

    Image 1 of 1: ‘styled button linking to the Zenodo entry for this lesson’

    Wrap-up

    Figure 1

    Image 1 of 1: ‘Chinese proverb 'The best time to plant a tree was 20 years ago. The second best time is now'’

    Figure 2

    Image 1 of 1: ‘Three 'help your team' principles: write FAIR, secure and maintainable code’

    Figure 3

    Image 1 of 1: ‘Three 'help your peers' principles: make your work reproducible, inclusive and credit everyone involved in it’

    Figure 4

    Image 1 of 1: ‘Three 'help the world principles': when developing research software be responsible, open and global, and humanist’
